regimeSub: FOMC split persists; Brent normalizes to $76 as OPEC+ expands while 4.2% CPI anchors hawkish Fed optionality.
themes: July 8 FOMC minutes confirm Fed officials split on rate direction with inflation concerns growing → Warsh retains September hike optionality as markets price 50% odds per TradingView
OPEC+ Jul 6 output expansion drives Brent to $76.42 (pre-Iran war levels) → energy disinflation tailwind materializing but core PCE at 3.4% keeps real policy restrictive
BOJ at 1% with tapering end FY2027 fails to arrest USDJPY at 161.45 → Japan negative real rates vs US 4.2% CPI sustains carry; gold at $4133 up $53 reflects persistent inflation hedge bid
June NFP miss at 57K vs est keeps labor market cooling narrative alive → weak participation rate at 5yr low complicates Fed calculus between inflation fight and growth concerns

signalNotes.name: US Real Rate
signalNotes.note: Policy rate 3.63% vs 4.2% CPI = -57bp real; restrictive in name only with core PCE at 3.4%

signalNotes.name: 2s10s Curve
signalNotes.note: 2Y 4.21% / 10Y 4.56% = +35bp steepening; term premium rising on fiscal and inflation uncertainty

signalNotes.name: HY Credit Spread
signalNotes.note: VIX at 15.84 suggests complacency; credit holding in despite stagflation crosscurrents

signalNotes.name: VIX
signalNotes.note: At 15.84 remarkably low given Fed split and sticky inflation; summer lull masking tail risks

signalNotes.name: Brent Crude
signalNotes.note: At $76.42 down from war highs as OPEC+ Jul 6 output expansion normalizes supply; geopolitical premium fading

signalNotes.name: Gold
signalNotes.note: At $4133 (+$53 vs yesterday) → inflation hedge demand persists as real rates remain negative

signalNotes.name: USD / KRW
signalNotes.note: At 1506 stable; BOK Jul 16 hike speculation live as Warsh-led Fed keeps hawkish bias intact

signalNotes.name: Semiconductor SOX
signalNotes.note: AI chip rebound per Jul 6 analyst calls; SK Hynix HBM lead over Samsung sustains KOSPI at 7556

headline: Fed remains split on rate direction as July FOMC minutes reveal growing inflation concerns, while OPEC+ output expansion normalizes oil prices and labor market cooling complicates Warsh's September decision calculus.
